ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the energy source (e.g., Coal, Lignite, Natural Gas) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the energy source */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the energy source (e.g., MWh, barrels) */,
  "energy_type" TEXT /* Type of energy source (renewable or non-renewable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "sector_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the sector (e.g., RES, COM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the region (e.g., NOR, SOU) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Year (e.g., 2010, 2011, ...) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Year the data belongs to */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Energy source of the data */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Sector the data belongs to */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Region the data belongs to */,
  "production" REAL /* Production amount */,
  "consumption" REAL /* Consumption amount */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (actual, estimated, forecasted) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES energy_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_data_points" (
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding energy data */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data point (production or consumption) */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of energy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_points_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES energy_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, policymaker, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the accessed energy data */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES energy_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user generating the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (PDF, CSV, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
